metadata.title: The Origins & Evolution of the GitHub MCP Registry

I'm probably using a new model you and I only actually I think met in person at build on our MCP panel

Don't do you want to give a little TLDR about what you're doing to get him sure

I am in charge of our MCP initiatives and so this is the GitHub MCP server
The new registry that we launched
I'm also on the MCP steering committee for the as a maintainer for the open source
MCP registry and we can talk today about how all of these things wire up
I'm relatively new. I started in December last year. So I'm coming up on my my first year
It's been an amazing journey and and James and I we're just setting about how much it's changed in this year, right?
Like we're moving
super fast now we have one of the most popular MCP servers in the world

And before that I was the VP of mobile for tweet deck and last of them and way back in today as an engineer at Apple
So it's been a been a long time in the industry
But to see these changes in the last 12 to 24 months
That's probably been the most exciting time in tech since I was like eight years old using a VIC 20 back in the day
It's so cool too because pretty much every product that you've worked on I've used so
Well, let's talk about MCP model context protocol now if people you know every blog with to say what it is
So Toby what is model context protocol and how are you using MCP servers today in your actual workflow?
So MCP model context protocol is a way for LLMs to interact
With the outside world and this means sort of primarily two things one is they can fetch
Context they can fetch data right and so if you think about how an LLM works
I had a cutoff date when it was trained and anything past that cutoff date or anything that was private won't be
accessible to the LLM so think of MCP as a way for it to go out and fetch either private information or more
Recent information you can say what are the top five things on Hacker News for instance and it could go go get that from like right now
The other thing it can do is it can create side effects it can interact with the outside world
So it can create a repo on GitHub
They can create a pull request that can create a figma file
so it's really the LLM's way to to interact and the history there's kind of interesting because
There was a function calling API for a lot of these models like opening
I had this function calling API and it was it was essentially the same thing
But it wasn't standardized it was sort of proprietary to each model and and thropic about a year ago now
Did the hard work of creating the standard and one of the ways they sort of bootstrapped the standard was they created like 20 or 30
Reference servers themselves
And one of those reference servers was the GitHub MCP server and they told us it was the most popular of all the reference servers
And so we took ownership of that from them and put it out there and just like we said
It's a way for LLM's not to interact with GitHub and and the GitHub platform
And you see a lot of these for a lot of services, right?
Like you're they're kind of wrapping rest API's or they're doing even better and they're putting agentic workflows together as as tools
The ones I use a lot are are the GitHub MCP server
I use play light right a lot as well and for people who aren't familiar with that
This is a Microsoft MCP server that gives LLM's of the ability to browse the web
And that's a super amazing thing right as like the LLM like driving through through websites and the way I use these is primarily through
The copilot coding agent and so James you and I were talking about this earlier today
There's a bunch of ways you can do agentic development, right? Like you can have an agent to do yes code or cursor
Caught code you can be on the command line. There's a bunch of different things you can do my preferred way is to use GitHub itself
And so for those are all who aren't familiar the copilot coding agent is a feature where you can assign an issue to copilot
And it turns away for like 20 minutes and it will come back and open up a pull request with what you wanted in there
And if you want to change that or it got something wrong you just leave comments to it
You're like hey at copilot, you know, I actually won 13 color themes instead of like the six that you added or you got this wrong
I want to use SQLite instead of MongoDB and then it'll go back and it will do another commit on the PR
Well, the copilot coding agent is mcp enabled and by default it has two mcp servers installed
One is the GitHub mcp server and the other is playwright
And it uses the GitHub mcp server for all of its interaction with GitHub
So as it's doing commits as it's looking through the history of files as it's looking at other reposts using
The GitHub mcp server there and then if you're building a web app
It uses playwright to like take screenshots of it to test the functionality that it implemented
And it's kind of incredible because a lot of the times you'll get back a pull request from the coding agent
And it'll have a screenshot of what it just built and it used playwright for that
And it validated at least a lot of the user flows through this web application by using playwrights
So for me the combination of those three things the coding agent the GitHub mcp server and the playwright mcp servers
Really unlocked some pretty amazing stuff
Yeah, that's super cool and the cool type part of mcp is like you were talking about is it's
It's not just for coding. It's in a bunch of different things right? So a bunch of different editors like i use vsvs code
But also there's different desktop apps that can connect to and that's the idea as it's a universal standard
That's out there. So when you go to the model context protocol website

Kind of viable like you know

A function a function to the LLM a text description of what that function does

So it's really easy to make these things and so it's all just an explosion of them and what you also saw as an explosion of of
registries to collect these things right because the problem is exactly what you said
Okay, now everyone's making mcp servers. Everyone's really excited about it
How do you find them and so a lot of just like third parties and independent developers and open source people started making
Registry like pulse mcp was one of them. I'm glam my AI was another one
But it became a problem for publishers right like we have the GitHub mcp server
We do a new release
How do we update these 12 or 13 registries that are out there? And there's this sort of more registries coming out every day
So the mcp steering committee got together and said, you know
Let's solve this and let's create a single point of truth
Where publishers can push their mcp servers into any updates for their mcp servers
We have all the information required to install these mcp servers in this API
And then a bunch of downstream registries can pull from this because our goal with this open source canonical
registry that we're building with mcp steering committee is not to be an end destination for users
It's a meant to be an end destination for publishers
So we published into this and then a bunch of downstream registries pull from this API and one of those registries is the GitHub
MCP registry doesn't pull from this yet because we're still in preview mode of the open source registry
But we're working with them and and I should say us because I'm on both sides of this equation
Yeah, you get
When you publish shows up in the GitHub mcp registry
So the GitHub mcp registry which we just launched two weeks ago now
Is a curated set of high quality servers just to bootstrap and get the thing out there
And then when we're confident that the open source registry is is done changing the API shape
We work through all the issues because we're really building it in public
Then we're going to sit they can have the mcp registry on top of it
And part of this flow is that you publish as a publisher in the open source registry
downstream registries github goose extensions marketplace cursor all these things pull from it
And then they enhance that data with platform specific information

It means displaying the readmees
It means one click installs in the VS code and VS code insiders and visual studio and an intelligent and all of our first-party platforms

and create a you can do

In it a server JSON
